feedback.text: Dear Commission Members, As a concerned European citizen, I urge you to include strong digital ownership and freedom protections in the upcoming Digital Fairness Act. While consumer fairness is an important goal, we must not ignore a growing and alarming threat: the increasing privatization of digital censorship under the guise of corporate policy. One key example is the role of payment infrastructure providers like Visa and Mastercard. Payment processors should process payments, but they have increasingly taken it upon themselves to decide what kinds of legal digital content are acceptable blocking payments to entire platforms based on vague moral judgments. This is not their role. Its a dangerous precedent: letting private financial networks dictate access to lawful digital goods and services, bypassing both public debate and legal systems. Moreover, we are watching with concern the developments in the United Kingdom under the Online Safety Act, where vague standards and overbroad enforcement mechanisms have created a chilling effect on expression and access to information. There is a real danger that the EU may follow a similar path under the banner of "safety" or "harm reduction", unintentionally creating a European version of the Great Firewall. Digital consumer rights must be more than refund policies or opt-in boxes. The right to retain access to legal digital content, the right to self-host, and the right to pay without ideological filtering are foundational to any fair digital market. A clear illustration of this need is the ongoing public backlash over the removal of video games from digital libraries even single-player or offline games simply because a publisher chooses to shut down access. The Stop Killing Games petition has gathered over 1.5 million signatures, demanding that consumers retain the right to play the games they bought. This shows how deeply the public values digital permanence and autonomy. I urge you to take this seriously. Digital fairness is not just about manipulative design or influencer marketing. Its about protecting access, ownership, and individual rights in a future where everything is digital. Please do not let a system evolve where private companies and vague regulations decide what Europeans are allowed to buy, access, or say. Thank you for your attention and commitment to real fairness in the digital age. Best regards, A concerned EU citizen
